title: Pseudognaphalium ephemerum
intro: Pseudognaphalium ephemerum, also known as annual cudweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pseudognaphalium ephemerum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pseudognaphalium ephemerum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Pseudognaphalium ephemerum is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pseudognaphalium, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. This classification places Pseudognaphalium ephemerum within the larger clade of dicotyledonous plants. More specifically, it is part of the order Asterales and the subclass Asteridae. Pseudognaphalium ephemerum is further classified as a species within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Pseudognaphalium ephemerum, a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, has been used in traditional medicine.
